'''Franck Maria''' (born 31 July 1997) is a French [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|pr}} or {{rlp|lf}} for the [[Catalans Dragons]] in the [[Betfred Super League]] and [[France national rugby league team|France]] at international level.

He has previously played for [[Saint-Estève XIII Catalan]], [[Villeneuve XIII RLLG|Villeneuve]] and [[FC Lézignan XIII|FC Lézignan]] in the [[Super XIII|Elite One Championship]]

==Background==
Maria was born in [[Villeneuve-sur-Lot]], France. He is the younger brother of former professional rugby league footballer [[Antoni Maria]].

He graduated through the [[Catalans Dragons]] Academy system.

==Career==
===Club career===
====Saint-Estève XIII Catalan====
Maria played for [[Saint-Estève XIII Catalan]] in the [[Super XIII|Elite One Championship]] between 2017 and 2019.

In 2018 he won the [[Lord Derby Cup 2017–2018|Lord Derby Cup]] when [[Saint-Estève XIII Catalan|Saint-Estève]] beat the [[Limoux Grizzlies|Limoux]] by 30–26 at the [[Stade Gilbert Brutus]], [[Perpignan]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.ffr13.fr/finale-de-la-coupe-de-france-2018-un-essai-en-or-de-thomas-ambert/ |title=Finale de la Coupe de France 2018 – Un essai en or de Thomas Ambert |publisher=FFRXIII |language=fr |date=11 June 2018 |access-date=10 January 2026}}</ref>

Maria was part of the team that won the [[Elite One Championship 2018–2019]], but was not part of match day squad in their Grand Final victory over [[AS Carcassonne]] in June 2019.<ref>{{cite news|title=Elite 1 : exploit de Saint-Estève/XIII Catalan qui devient champion de France face à Carcassonne XIII (32-24)|url=https://www.lindependant.fr/2019/06/29/elite-1-suivez-en-direct-la-finale-entre-saint-estevexiii-catalan-et-carcassonne-xiii,8286062.php|website=L'Independant|date=29 June 2019}}</ref>

====Villeneuve====
He played for [[Villeneuve XIII RLLG|Villeneuve]] in the [[Elite One Championship 2019–2020|2019–2020 Elite One Championship]] before his spell at the club was cut short by Covid-19.

====FC Lézignan====
Maria played for [[FC Lézignan]] in the Elite One Championship between 2021 and 2023.

====Saint-Estève XIII====
In 2023 he returned to the [[Catalans Dragons]] feeder side [[Saint-Estève XIII Catalan]], playing in the [[Super XIII]] whilst being part of their [[Super League]] setup.

Maria 2025 he won the [[Coupe de France Lord Derby]] when [[Saint-Estève XIII Catalan|Saint-Estève XIII]] beat [[Racing Club Albi XIII|Racing Club Albi]] by 26–18 at the [[Parc des Sports et de l'Amitié]], [[Narbonne]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://treizemondial.fr/saint-esteve-xiii-catalan-fait-plier-albi-et-remporte-la-coupe-de-france/ |title=Saint-Estève XIII Catalan bends Albi and wins the Coupe de France |website=Treize Mondial |date=5 April 2025 |access-date=10 January 2026}}</ref>

====Catalans Dragons====
In March 2024 made his [[Super League]] debut the [[Catalans Dragons]], coming off the bench in a home victory over the [[Castleford Tigers]].

Maria was given a heavy ban following a [[Penalty card#Red_card|red card]] against the [[Salford Red Devils]] in September 2025.<ref>[https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/catalans-dragons-forward-discovers-fate-after-horror-tackle-as-big-ban-received Catalans Dragons forward discovers fate after horror tackle as big ban received]</ref><ref>[https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/hull-fc-castleford-tigers-salford-red-devils-catalans-dragons-men-banned-as-review-panel-issue-23-charges-5307948 Hull FC, Castleford Tigers, Salford Red Devils & Catalans Dragons men banned as review panel issue 23 charges]</ref>

===International career===
Maria played for [[France national rugby league team|France B]] in October 2022, featuring in their 18–16 loss against the [[England Knights]].

He made his full international debut for [[France national rugby league team|France]] in September 2023, coming off the bench in their 78–10 victory over [[Serbia national rugby league team|Serbia]]. He earned his cap the following June, in a 40-8 loss against [[England national rugby league team|England]].
